notes: The restaurant-linked four-page dine-in PDF publishes 99 standalone products across 15 consumer categories; the visible owner-issued Wix ordering menu contains a 90-product subset.

All 110 frozen rows were reconciled: 94 exact products, one normalized Chicken 65 product, ten parser artifacts, and five superseded Wix-inventory products. The 95 real rows map one-to-one to 95 current products.
The corrected catalog restores Mint & Coriander, Tamarind, Matter Pulao, and Gulab Jamoon. Nine PDF-backed dine-in products remain valid even though they are not selectable online.
All 95 matched frozen rows had incorrect generic categories or menu presentation. House Dressings is retained only as a salad subheading, not a product.

Aroma publishes restaurant-issued menu descriptions with positive ingredient wording, but no complete allergen matrix, allergen-free assurance, or item-level cross-contact disclosure was found on the owner site, current PDF, ordering menu, or targeted official-domain search.

Positive fixed signals are limited to restaurant-named ingredients and unavoidable named formulations. Missing ingredient terms are never inverted into negative claims, and mayContain remains empty for all 99 products.

Seventeen frozen allergen outcomes were wrong: examples include missed milk from raita-served biryanis, missed milk and cashew on Gajjar Halwa, missed wheat/gluten/soy on Hakka Noodles, false milk from the adjective creamy on Sarso Ka Saag, and false tree-nut promotion from coconut on Bagara Baigan and Coco Mussel Curry.

The corrected catalog contains 63 restaurant-issued positive ingredient rows, 36 accurately unavailable rows, and four separately labeled Ingredient Intelligence risks for unspecified batter or flavor wording.

The frozen generic extraction mixed site boilerplate, event pages, menu headings, a dressing heading, and unreferenced Wix inventory into the food catalog; it also omitted four current products and collapsed every real product into Items or indian.

The reproducible audit parser authenticates to Aroma's owner-issued Wix menu APIs, enforces the exact two-menu visibility contract, uses the owner-linked PDF to authorize the complete 99-product dine-in boundary, and requires the 90-product visible ordering menu to be a subset.
The parser fails closed if menu visibility, product counts, source IDs, categories, dine-in-only products, or captured source hashes change.
Twelve focused catalog, reconciliation, and repaired-output tests pass. Two target-only repair runs produced the same target hash and left the non-target restaurant projection unchanged.

sourceAttempts.outcome: The exact Fairfax restaurant page at 12821 Fair Lakes Parkway links directly to a current four-page dine-in PDF. The captured PDF contains 99 products across 15 categories and no complete allergen or cross-contact matrix.
notes: The PDF was rendered to four PNG pages and visually checked against extracted text before catalog construction.

sourceAttempts.outcome: The restaurant's owner-issued Wix ordering surface exposes 90 visible products. Authenticated menu, section, and item API captures contain two menus, 30 sections, and 220 inventory records; the visible ordering menu is a strict subset of the PDF-backed 99-product dine-in catalog.
notes: Hidden and unreferenced Wix inventory was not accepted as current unless the owner-linked PDF independently publishes the product.

sourceAttempts.outcome: Targeted official-domain searches for allergen, allergy, cross-contact, and PDF disclosures found the current menu and ingredient descriptions but no restaurant-issued allergen matrix, complete ingredient guide, negative allergen claim, or cross-contact statement.

findings.summary: The frozen 110-row catalog included ten non-product artifacts and five superseded Wix-inventory products while omitting four current products.
findings.resolution: Rebuilt the exact 99-product current catalog, removed all 15 invalid rows, restored all four omissions, and preserved the nine PDF-backed dine-in-only products.

findings.summary: Every one of the 95 real matched frozen rows used a generic Items or indian category or otherwise failed to preserve the current menu presentation.
findings.resolution: Assigned all 99 products to their 15 restaurant-published categories and retained current restaurant-issued names and descriptions.

findings.summary: Seventeen frozen products had missing or invented fixed allergen signals, including false coconut-to-tree-nut and creamy-to-milk mappings and missed explicitly named wheat, soy, milk, and nut ingredients.
findings.resolution: Re-reviewed every current description, corrected all 17 outcomes and ten provenance states, kept unsupported risks separate, and added no negative or cross-contact claim.

findings.summary: Using only the visible ordering menu would incorrectly remove nine products that remain published on the restaurant's current dine-in PDF.
findings.resolution: Defined the current catalog as the PDF-backed 99-product dine-in menu and treated the 90 orderable products as a strict presentation subset.

repairs.summary: Rebuilt Aroma's current PDF-backed menu, repaired all product boundaries, categories, omissions, and allergen semantics, and published the corrected 99-product target without changing another restaurant.

evidence.excerpt: Authenticated owner-issued Wix capture: 2 menus, 30 sections, 220 inventory records, a 99-product PDF-backed dine-in contract after excluding House Dressings, and a 90-product visible ordering subset.
